title: ADR-014: Adopt Event Sourcing for Order Pipeline
context: The order pipeline currently uses synchronous database writes across three services. Peak traffic causes contention and makes audit trails difficult to reconstruct. Product requires immutable order history for compliance.
decision: Adopt event sourcing for the order aggregate with an outbox pattern for downstream consumers. New writes go to an append-only event log; read models are rebuilt asynchronously.
consequences: Positive: full audit trail, reduced cross-service locking, easier replay for debugging. Negative: increased operational complexity, team training required, migration window for existing orders.

alternatives.title: Continue with synchronous writes
alternatives.description: Minimal change but does not solve audit or contention issues at scale.

alternatives.title: Change Data Capture (CDC) only
alternatives.description: Lower migration cost but provides eventual consistency without domain-level event semantics.

alternatives.title: Full CQRS with separate write/read stores
alternatives.description: Strong separation but higher infrastructure cost than event sourcing with projections.
